rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative selenocyclization of 2-vinylbenzamides with diphenyl diselenide to form selenium-containing isobenzofuran-imines (3a-type products) in a flow microreactor.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of diphenyl diselenide (PhSeSePh, 2a) to a cationic radical intermediate that fragments to PhSe radical and PhSe cation; later, anodic oxidation of cyclized intermediate E to cation F

key_intermediates.name: alkyl radical D (benzylic C-radical bearing SePh after PhSe• addition to vinyl)
cyclized intermediate E (isobenzofuran-type with N-H, after intramolecular O-cyclization of D)
intermediate G (cation adduct from PhSe+ + 1a, path b alternative)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes PhSeSePh (2a) to radical cation A, which fragments to PhSe• (B) and PhSe+ (C). PhSe• adds to the vinyl group of 2-vinylbenzamide 1a to give alkyl radical D, which cyclizes intramolecularly via the amide oxygen to E; E is further oxidized at the anode to cation F, which is deprotonated to give product 3a. Cathode reduces protons to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction significantly suppressed; yield of 3a dropped to 10% with 3 equiv

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Yield of 3a reduced to 30% with 3 equiv

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Yield of 3a reduced to 40% with 3 equiv, supporting radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: diphenyl diselenide (2a)
probe_reagents.observation: CV shows oxidation peak at 1.84 V, lower than 1a at 2.25 V, indicating 2a is preferentially oxidized at the anode

probe_reagents.name: 2-vinylbenzamide 1a
probe_reagents.observation: CV oxidation peak at 2.25 V, higher than 2a

notes: Two pathways proposed: favored path a (PhSe• radical addition to alkene → alkyl radical D → cyclization → anodic oxidation to F → product) and alternative path b in which PhSe+ cation C reacts with 1a to give cation G, undergoing intramolecular nucleophilic cyclization to F and then dehydrogenation to 3a. The authors cannot exclude path b.
